sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: The viz dashboard is a browser-based interface for exploring the cortex knowledge graph. It provides five views: Graph, List, Workflow, Tests, and Docs — all reading from the same cortex index database.

sections.heading: Quick Start
sections.content:
```bash
# Build the index first
cortex build .

# Launch the dashboard (opens browser automatically)
cortex viz .

# Launch on a specific port without auto-open
cortex viz . --port 9000 --no-browser
```

The dashboard runs at `http://localhost:8741` by default.

sections.heading: Core Functionality
sections.content:
### Views
- **Graph view** — Interactive Cytoscape.js visualization. Nodes are colored by type; border rings show staleness. Click a node to open the detail panel. Use the depth slider and focus mode to explore neighborhoods.
- **List view** — Sortable table of all nodes. Columns: title, type, location, staleness, tags. Click a row to open detail.
- **Workflow view** — Explore dFlow-decorated `@workflow` and `@task` functions. See steps, critical annotations, and source code in a Monaco editor.
- **Tests view** — Browse `@test_workflow` and `@test_suite` functions. Filter by tier (T1/T2/T3), result status (pass/fail), and fixtures. JUnit results shown when available.
- **Docs view** — Browse and edit DocJSON files. Folder tree navigation, section-level editing, link picker for connecting prose to code nodes, and Mermaid diagram preview.

### Sidebar Filters
The sidebar provides filters for node type, tags, staleness status, and edge types. The "Hide orphans" toggle removes nodes with no visible edges after filtering.

### Focus Mode
Enter a node ID in the focus input to center the graph on that node's ego-network. Control depth (1-5 hops) and direction (in/out/both).

### Rescan
Click the Rescan button to re-index the project without restarting the server.

sections.heading: Configuration & Options
sections.content:
| Option | Description | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `--port PORT` | HTTP port for the dashboard | 8741 |
| `--no-browser` | Don't auto-open browser on launch | Auto-open |

The dashboard auto-detects large projects (>5000 nodes) and starts in list view to avoid slow graph rendering. Switch to graph view manually when ready.

LocalStorage keys used by the frontend:
- `cortex-doc-wide` — docs wide mode
- `cortex-doc-folders` — expanded folder state (session)
- `cortex-workflow-id` — last selected workflow ID (session)
- `cortex-test-id` — last selected test ID (session)

sections.heading: Best Practices & Troubleshooting
sections.content:
### Best Practices
- Run `cortex build .` before launching viz to ensure the database is current
- Use focus mode to explore dense graphs — full graph rendering can be slow at scale
- Use the Staleness filter to triage documentation debt

### Common Issues
- **Problem:** Graph is blank after launching
- **Solution:** No nodes in database. Run `cortex build .` first.

- **Problem:** Graph renders very slowly
- **Solution:** Large project. Use list view or focus mode. The app auto-detects this and warns.

- **Problem:** DocJSON changes not appearing
- **Solution:** Click Rescan or refresh the page. The server reads docs from disk on each request.
